description_long: This object is a robust stone archway bollard, constructed from large, roughly hewn stone blocks with a weathered, dark gray surface. The structure consists of two vertical pillars supporting a semi-circular arch made from wedge-shaped stones, each block featuring visible seams and a slightly irregular texture. The base of each pillar is wider and appears to be made from stacked, uneven stone slabs, providing stability and a rugged aesthetic. The arch is composed of several voussoirs, culminating in a keystone at the apex, which is slightly larger and more prominent than the others. The surface of the stone is mottled with subtle variations in color, suggesting exposure to the elements and age. This type of bollard would typically serve as a decorative or symbolic access control barrier in urban parks, plazas, or historical districts.
description: A heavy, weathered stone archway bollard with two sturdy pillars and a semi-circular arch of wedge-shaped blocks. The surface is dark gray, rough, and mottled, giving it an ancient, durable appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile of the archway, with the layered stone base and the thickness of the arch blocks visible.
description_view_1: Frontal view displaying the full archway, the keystone at the top, and the symmetrical arrangement of the stone pillars and base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, highlighting the depth and blocky construction of the arch and base.
description_view_3: Rear view of the archway, mirroring the front but with less emphasis on the keystone and more on the stone texture and seams.
